llm_result.summary: Modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers tailored to panic with public exposure: rapid autonomic arousal, chest tightness, fear of fainting, social evaluation concerns, safety-checking and escape behaviors, perceived unpredictability, and heightened attention to internal cues. Eight levers target distinct, changeable predictors (across biological regulation, cognitive-behavioral skills, and social environment) to reduce episode frequency/intensity and improve tolerance in public settings, with cross-domain synergies (e.g., breathing and interoception training augmenting CBT reframing; social exposure complemented by attention retraining). Each lever links to specific criteria (C01–C08), with practical measurement ideas and implementation timelines.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured paced diaphragmatic breathing with short exhale optimization and box-breathing at onset, paired with brief cognitive check-ins to dampen catastrophic appraisals
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Physiological arousal dampening reduces rapid onset of panic, chest tightness intensity, and salience of internal cues; concurrent cognitive check-ins limit automatic threat interpretations and attentional focus on bodily sensations.
solutions.measurement: Episode frequency per week, peak anxiety rating (0-10) during episodes, chest tightness intensity (0-10), self-reported breathing rate during onset
solutions.safety_notes: Provide proper instruction; stop if hyperventilation occurs; monitor for dizziness; advise medical eval if chest pain worsens
solutions.mapping_query: S01 maps to C01-C02-C04-C08-C07; breathing and arousal regulation during panic onset.

solutions.solution_candidate: Behavioral experiments to gradually reduce pulse-checking and safety-signal checking; replace with alternative grounding and tolerance strategies; implement urge-surfing cycles
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing safety behaviors weakens safety-seeking reinforcement, lowers attention focus on internal cues, and decreases anticipatory anxiety during episodes, improving tolerance in public settings.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of pulse checks per episode, time to first panic after onset, self-rated urge to check (0-10), use of grounding (yes/no)
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for excessive distress during exposure; ensure gradual steps and clinician supervision if needed
solutions.mapping_query: S02 targets C05 and related avoidance/safety behaviors (C01, C04, C08, C07) as therapy reduces safety checks.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured interoceptive exposure to chest tightness, dizziness, and heartbeat sensations in a controlled, Safe context; couple with cognitive reappraisal during exposure
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Habituation to internal sensations reduces fear of bodily cues and misinterpretation as medical catastrophe; promotes tolerance of sensations without avoidance.
solutions.measurement: Number and intensity of interoceptive exposure sessions, self-reported fear during exposures (0-10), heart-rate/respiration changes if available
solutions.safety_notes: Proceed under supervision; avoid hyperventilation; medical clearance if chest symptoms worsen
solutions.mapping_query: S03 maps to C01-C02-C03-C07-C08 via interoceptive habituation to bodily sensations.

solutions.solution_candidate: Psychoeducation module explaining panic cycle, realistic appraisal of fainting risk, cognitive restructuring of catastrophic interpretations, and scheduled worry/solution focused discussions
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhancing understanding of physiological signals and challenging catastrophic beliefs reduces anticipatory anxiety, embarrassment concerns, and perceived unpredictability.
solutions.measurement: Belief in fainting probability (0-100%), pre/post worry intensity, social embarrassment rating
solutions.safety_notes: Content should be delivered sensitively; validate personal concerns and adapt to cultural context
solutions.mapping_query: S04 aligns with C03-C04-C01-C07 through CBT psychoeducation and belief modification.

solutions.solution_candidate: Attention retraining to decenter from internal sensations; implement grounding routines (5-4-3-2-1, sensory anchoring) and short mindfulness micro-practices during and between episodes
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Shifting attention away from internal cues reduces salience of sensations and social evaluative concerns, lowering episode intensity and embarrassment.
solutions.measurement: Use of grounding techniques per week; self-reported attention to internal cues during episodes (0-10); episode intensity
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; ensure technique accessibility in public settings
solutions.mapping_query: S05 targets C08 and related aspects via attention retraining and grounding.

solutions.solution_candidate: Graduated, therapist-guided social exposures in public contexts; combine with scripts and peer-support; practice tolerating symptoms without avoidance
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Confronting social-evaluative scenarios reduces embarrassment concerns and unpredictability beliefs; improves tolerance of symptoms in public without resorting to escape
solutions.measurement: Number of social exposure tasks completed; self-rated embarrassment in public (0-10); frequency of panic in public
solutions.safety_notes: Proceed with therapist; monitor for escalations; ensure safety planning for public settings
solutions.mapping_query: S06 maps to C04-C01-C07 via social exposure and evaluative processing.

solutions.solution_candidate: Develop a non-escape coping plan with a delay (e.g., 3-minute pause) and alternative strategies (grounding, safe space normalization); reduce impulse to exit public spaces
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing automatic escape responses weakens reinforcement of avoidance, increasing tolerance and reducing distress in public episodes
solutions.measurement: Frequency of exiting episodes, time to first coping response (minutes), self-rated urge to exit (0-10)
solutions.safety_notes: Need gradual steps; escalate to clinician if episodes escalate beyond coping capacity
solutions.mapping_query: S07 targets C06 via exposure to coping strategies and reduces avoidance (C01/C04/C07) by tolerating episodes.

solutions.solution_candidate: Optimize sleep quality, regular aerobic activity, hydration, and circadian rhythm alignment to lower baseline arousal and improve autonomic regulation
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep and physical health reduce baseline anxiety susceptibility and sensitivity to bodily cues; better regulatory capacity supports social situations
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality (sleep diary or PSQI score), weekly aerobic minutes, daily water intake, morning HRV (if available)
solutions.safety_notes: None; check with healthcare provider for medical contraindications to exercise
solutions.mapping_query: S08 links to C01-C02-C07-C08-C04 via lifestyle-biological regulation and circadian health.
